reports.title: HbA1c Panel
reports.aiSummary: Your HbA1c is 7.8%, a little above the ~7% goal often used when managing diabetes (HbA1c is your average blood sugar over the past ~3 months). It is reassuring that it is not far from target, and small changes often bring it down. This is one snapshot — your doctor reads it alongside your full picture.
doctorQuestions: What HbA1c target is right for me?

summary: Meera lives with Type 2 Diabetes (diagnosed 2021) and takes Metformin 500mg twice daily. Her most recent HbA1c is 7.8%, a little above the ~7% goal often used in diabetes care, and her recent cholesterol and thyroid checks are broadly in their usual ranges. She has a penicillin allergy (rash). These are plain-language explanations of her own records, not a diagnosis — her doctor reads them alongside her full picture.
